assertions.title: GPU crossover documentation exists with measured numbers

steps.observed: File exists, 3375 chars
Contains 1MB, 10MB, 100MB, 1GB (4 sizes)
States 'No crossover was found' with per-size timing table
RTX 4070 vs rg at 1MB: +3.481s; never amortized away

steps.action: Check when GPU should/shouldn't be used
steps.observed: Sections 'Should use GPU' (not for current host) and 'Shouldn't use GPU' (general text search)

assertions.title: Invalid device ID produces clear error

steps.observed: Exit code 1, stderr: 'Requested GPU device IDs [99] are not available to the sidecar. Available device IDs: [0, 1].'

steps.action: Check for stack trace or panic
steps.observed: Clean error message, no panic, no stack trace

steps.action: Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" and run tg search --gpu-device-ids 0 "ERROR" bench_data
steps.expected: Non-zero exit code, clear CUDA error message
steps.observed: Exit code 1, stderr: 'CUDA is unavailable for requested GPU device IDs [0]: CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES is empty, so no GPUs are visible to the sidecar.'
Clean user-facing error message, no traceback

steps.action: Create hanging_sidecar.py that reads stdin then sleeps 300s
steps.observed: Script created in artifacts/val-gpu/

steps.action: Run tg with TG_SIDECAR_SCRIPT=hanging_sidecar.py and TG_SIDECAR_TIMEOUT_MS=3000
steps.expected: Timeout within ~3s, non-zero exit, clear error
steps.observed: Elapsed 3.03s, exit code 124, stderr: 'Python sidecar timed out after 3000 ms and was terminated'
No hanging_sidecar processes found; only pre-existing unrelated python processes

steps.action: Verify default timeout is <= 30s in code
steps.observed: DEFAULT_SIDECAR_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000 in python_sidecar.rs
--gpu-auto not in help output; --gpu-device-ids present

steps.expected: Document explains why --gpu-auto was not added
steps.observed: States: 'the benchmark never produced a row where GPU was at least 20% faster than rg'
gpu_auto_recommendation.should_add_flag = false, reason: 'No measured GPU/device row beat rg by at least 20% at any corpus size.'

assertions.title: GPU search correctness parity with CPU

steps.action: Attempt live GPU search: tg search --gpu-device-ids 0 "ERROR" bench_data
steps.observed: Exit code 1: 'CuDF and Torch GPU backends were unavailable'
bench_gpu_scale.json has 3 correctness checks (RTX 4070, 10MB corpus): all PASS with matching counts and file sets

evidence.consoleErrors: GPU backends unavailable in current environment

assertions.issues: Cannot perform live GPU search: 'CuDF and Torch GPU backends were unavailable'. Historical benchmark data in bench_gpu_scale.json shows correctness parity for 3 patterns on RTX 4070.

assertions.title: GPU benchmarks cover multiple corpus sizes

steps.expected: Entries for >= 4 corpus sizes with timing data
steps.observed: 4 rows: 1MB, 10MB, 100MB, 1GB - each with rg, tg_cpu, and per-GPU timing
Supports --corpus-sizes arg (comma-separated), default: 1MB,10MB,100MB,1GB

steps.expected: median_s, samples_s per size per backend
steps.observed: Each row has rg.median_s, tg_cpu.median_s, and gpu[].median_s with status and samples

assertions.title: GPU sidecar malformed output handled gracefully

steps.action: Create malformed_sidecar.py that returns valid envelope with non-JSON stdout
steps.observed: Script created: returns stdout='THIS IS NOT JSON AT ALL {{{broken'

steps.action: Run tg search --gpu-device-ids 0 --json "ERROR" bench_data with TG_SIDECAR_SCRIPT=malformed_sidecar.py
steps.expected: Non-zero exit, clear error, no panic
steps.observed: Exit code 1, stderr: 'Error: GPU sidecar returned malformed search JSON payload: expected {total_matches, total_files, matches[]} with string file/text fields and integer line_number values (expected value at line 1 column 1)'

steps.expected: No panic or raw parse-error stack trace
steps.observed: No panic, no traceback - clean user-facing error message

blockers.description: GPU Python backends (CuDF and Torch) are unavailable in the current environment. The .venv_cuda environment exists but the main venv lacks GPU dependencies.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Attempted direct GPU search with default Python; received 'CuDF and Torch GPU backends were unavailable'. This is a known pre-existing issue documented in AGENTS.md.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: 7 passed, 0 failed, 1 blocked (VAL-GPU-006: GPU backends unavailable for live correctness test, historical benchmark data shows parity).
